reasoning: The Lean signature captures exactly the Munkres exercise: Y is linearly ordered (matching Munkres' "ordered set" = simple/total order) with the order topology via `[LinearOrder Y] [OrderTopology Y]` (the Mathlib class that asserts the topology is generated by open rays — the textbook definition of the order topology); f, g : X → Y are continuous; and the conclusion `IsClosed {x | f x ≤ g x}` is the exact set-closed assertion. The hypothesis `[TopologicalSpace X]` is just the ambient structure that the informal statement presupposes when saying "closed in X". No hypothesis has been added or dropped, the quantifier structure is correctly universal over (X, Y, f, g), and the types match the informal content. This is a logically equivalent formalization.
